name: X25519MLKEM768 KEM-wire KAT
provenance: Standards-derived, non-circular. ML-KEM-768 values are from NIST ACVP (FIPS 203 final). X25519 values are from RFC 7748 §6.1. NONE of these were generated by an N-PAMP implementation. A consuming implementation that reproduces these is being checked against the standards, not against another N-PAMP build.
purpose: Pin the X25519MLKEM768 hybrid-KEM WIRE BYTE ORDER (the combiner-order surface, ADR-0005) and the component encodings, so the wire-byte-order bug that symmetric self-interop cannot catch is caught by a standards-anchored vector in EVERY conforming implementation.

mlkem768_keygen.description: Deterministic ML-KEM-768 key generation from the FIPS 203 seed (d, z). NewDecapsulationKey768(d||z).EncapsulationKey().Bytes() MUST equal ek. Verified reproducible against Go crypto/mlkem (final FIPS 203).
mlkem768_keygen.source: NIST ACVP-Server, ML-KEM-keyGen-FIPS203, parameterSet ML-KEM-768, tgId 2, tcId 26
mlkem768_keygen.seed_form: Go crypto/mlkem NewDecapsulationKey768 expects a 64-byte seed in d||z form (d = seed[0:32], z = seed[32:64]).

x25519_rfc7748_6_1.description: RFC 7748 §6.1 Curve25519 Diffie-Hellman test vector. NewPrivateKey(alice_private).PublicKey().Bytes() MUST equal alice_public; ECDH(alice_private, bob_public) MUST equal shared_secret (and symmetrically for bob). crypto/ecdh applies RFC 7748 clamping internally, so the raw private scalars reproduce the published outputs.

mlkem768_decaps_reference.description: NIST ML-KEM-768 encapsulation/decapsulation known-answer: ciphertext c decapsulates to shared secret K under this record's key (deterministic encaps randomness m). This is the NIST anchor for the ML-KEM SHARED SECRET. NOTE: ACVP supplies this record's decapsulation key as the EXPANDED 2400-byte dk, and Go's public crypto/mlkem imports ONLY a 64-byte seed — so this leg is NOT executable via the Go public API. It IS executable by implementations that can import an expanded decapsulation key, and K is reused as the ML-KEM shared secret in combined_secret below.
mlkem768_decaps_reference.source: NIST ACVP-Server, ML-KEM-encapDecap-FIPS203, parameterSet ML-KEM-768, function encapsulation, testType AFT, tgId 2, tcId 26

wire_layout.description: How an implementation MUST assemble the on-wire fields from the components above, per spec/10 §4 (ML-KEM-first, ADR-0005). These are the assertions a conforming implementation must satisfy.
wire_layout.kem_ciphertext: ml-kem ciphertext (1088) || server x25519 public (32) = 1120 octets (TLV 0x08). ML-KEM-first.
wire_layout.combined_secret: ml-kem shared secret (32) || x25519 shared secret (32) = 64 octets, the raw IKM to HKDF-Extract (spec §4/§5.2). ML-KEM-first. For this vector: combined_secret = mlkem768_decaps_reference.shared_secret_K || x25519_rfc7748_6_1.shared_secret.

go_public_executable: kem_share order: assembled ek||alice_public, ML-KEM-first (NIST ek + RFC pub)

kem_ciphertext order: client DeriveSharedSecrets of (ct||bob_public) recovers the X25519 shared secret == RFC K (RFC-anchored; the ct here is generated by encapsulating to ek, so the ML-KEM shared-secret VALUE on this leg is round-trip, not NIST-anchored)
combined_secret/IKM order: HKDF-Extract IKM is ML-KEM_SS || X25519_SS, not the reverse
not_go_public_executable: mlkem768_decaps_reference: decapsulating the NIST ciphertext c to the NIST K requires importing the record's EXPANDED 2400-byte dk; Go's public crypto/mlkem imports only the 64-byte seed. Runnable by implementations that import an expanded dk. Until a final-FIPS-203 seed-based (d,z + c + K) vector is published, the NIST anchor for the ML-KEM shared-secret VALUE is carried as data here and is not executed by the Go reference impl.
coverage.disconfirmed_candidate: C2SP/CCTV intermediate/ML-KEM-768.txt is a single-source coherent (d,z,ek,c,K) vector but is FIPS 203 INITIAL PUBLIC DRAFT era (Dec 2023, Algorithm 12/13 XOF input-ordering swap); it does NOT reproduce under final-FIPS-203 crypto/mlkem (ek and K both mismatch) and MUST NOT be used as a final-standard KAT.
